title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: The 'No Kings' movement, opposing Donald Trump, sparked global protests, including events in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam.
Over 3,000 'No Kings' protests are planned across the U.S., making it potentially the largest protest in U.S. history.
Massachusetts hosts over 160 rallies, the second highest in the country after California, according to organizers.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
The show features Alicia Carmody and Liz McGraw, prominent figures in Rhode Island's culinary and cannabis industries.
The series explores friendships, feuds, and social dynamics among Rhode Islanders with deep community roots.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department to cut 50 positions to save up to $4 million amid budget cuts.
The department faces a $26.5 million budget reduction impacting police presence in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, and Palmer.
Layoffs attributed to funding issues tied to legislative deficits since 1999's abolition of county government.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy allocated $2.65 million to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals linked to cancer risks.
The new funding will provide over 270 firefighters with two sets of PFAS-free gear each.
Massachusetts plans to ban sales of firefighting gear with added PFAS starting January.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley executive produced the 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary, airing March 30-31 on PBS.
The film features narrations by George Clooney and voices from Jeff Goldblum, Ted Danson, and Meryl Streep.
Henley founded The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to preserve land tied to Thoreau, supporting environmental stewardship and social responsibility.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics attended the France vs. Brazil friendly match at Gillette Stadium, with Jayson Tatum doing the coin toss.
France won the match 2-1 with a goal from Kylian Mbappe, who swapped jerseys with Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez.
Coach Joe Mazzulla noted similarities between soccer and basketball, emphasizing possession and counterattacks.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored 36 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-102 victory over the Atlanta Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists despite shooting 8-for-24 from the field.
The Celtics capitalized on a controversial foul call involving Payton Pritchard and Nickeil Alexander-Walker late in the game.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles’ tendon, motivated by the team's success.
Despite being below his shooting averages, Tatum is strong on rebounds, averaging 9.2 per game since his return.
With Tatum back, the Celtics are holding second place and preparing for a potential home playoff series.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard on Friday afternoon.
The incident followed a brief altercation between two men, both allegedly armed, who knew each other.
The shooting is under investigation by the Middlesex DA’s Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 following purchase by the Houston Rockets' owners, the Fertitta family.
The WNBA franchise will be renamed the Houston Comets, in honor of the city's former women's basketball team.
The reported $300 million sale marks a WNBA record and follows a blocked attempt by Steve Pagliuca to move the team to Boston.
